title: INF-9 Cross-session state restore (SSM state persistence to disk + rehydration)
goal: Implement persist_to_disk + load_from_disk for SSMStateSnapshot per refinement INF-9 feature_parity_detail: 'cross-session state restore (state-vector persistence + restore)'. SSM state survives Handshake process restart; operator can resume a long-running RWKV chat from a saved state.
expected_diff_shape: Edit src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/candle/state_vector.rs to add persist_to_disk/load_from_disk + StateVectorArtifact metadata via ArtifactStore; tests.
unblock_conditions: MT-088 state-vector primitive + MT-114 prefix-reuse merged.
implementation_notes: Per refinement INF-9 feature_parity_detail. persist_to_disk(state_id, dest_path): serialize SSMStateSnapshot via bincode or candle Tensor to_vec; write `{ artifact: bytes, metadata: StateVectorPersistMetadata }` to ArtifactStore; mint StateVectorArtifactId v7. StateVectorPersistMetadata: `{ state_id, model_id, model_artifact_sha256, variant: SSMVariant, n_tokens_advanced, persisted_at_utc, persisted_by: OperatorId, content_hash, license_tag }`. load_from_disk(artifact_id) -> Result<SSMStateSnapshot, ModelRuntimeError>: read metadata, verify variant matches target model, verify model_artifact_sha256 matches the currently-loaded model's sha256 (or warn + fail per operator policy), deserialize tensors, validate content_hash. Failure modes: variant mismatch -> Err; sha256 mismatch -> Err with explicit guidance ("snapshot was taken against a different model artifact; either load that artifact or recapture"); deserialization error -> Err. ArtifactStore integration: persist_to_disk goes through KernelActionCatalogV1 `kernel.subquadratic.persist_state` action -> WriteBoxV1. Tests: persist on Mamba2, simulate process restart by dropping the model+state and reloading, load_from_disk, generate continuation; assert deterministic. Sha256-mismatch test. Variant-mismatch test.
risk_if_missed: INF-9 cross-session restore missing; SSM state ephemeral; operator E-2 breached.
validator_focus: Process-restart simulation test; sha256 + variant validation enforced; mutations through KernelActionCatalogV1.

lifecycle.resume_reason: prior session left in-process bytes-in/bytes-out surface but explicitly deferred ArtifactStore + KernelActionCatalogV1 + WriteBoxV1 integration to a non-existent follow-on MT; resuming to land the full MT-117 contract (disk persistence + load_from_disk + catalog action) in this MT per Spec-Realism Gate sub-rule 1 (no deferred-live escape)

implementation_record.summary: MT-117 landed the disk-integration layer the prior session deferred: persist_to_artifact_store + load_from_artifact_store + load_from_artifact_store_into_handle in src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/candle/state_vector.rs; new kernel.subquadratic.persist_state catalog action in src/backend/handshake_core/src/kernel/action_catalog.rs; candle/mod.rs re-exports for the new surface; 11 new disk-integration tests appended to tests/inf9_cross_session_state_tests.rs covering the marquee process-restart-via-artifact-store path plus adversarial scenarios (unknown artifact_id, tampered payload, corrupted manifest, cross-handle sha/variant mismatch, unbound prefix handle, concurrent persist). The obsolete STATE_VECTOR_PERSIST_DISK_DEFERRED_MARKER constant and its self-referential test were removed because the deferred work is complete in-MT.

spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_1_no_deferred_live_escape: PASS — the prior 'follow-on' deferral was the exact pattern this gate forbids; resuming the MT and implementing the deferred surface in the same MT resolves it. No *Unavailable / not yet wired / pending MT-NNN code paths remain in the MT-117 surface.
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_2_external_resource_touch: PASS — persist_to_artifact_store writes a real envelope file under <workspace>/.handshake/artifacts/L3/<uuid>/{payload,artifact.json} via write_file_artifact (atomic temp+fsync+rename); load_from_artifact_store reads and validates the manifest content_hash against the on-disk bytes before deserializing. Tests use tempfile::TempDir as workspace_root and verify the actual filesystem layout.
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_3_implementer_cannot_self_certi: PASS — lifecycle.claimed_by (KERNEL_BUILDER-20260523-031814) is distinct from lifecycle.completed_by (NULL — only the validator role can set this; KERNEL_BUILDER transitions only to READY_FOR_VALIDATION).

files_touched: src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/candle/state_vector.rs (added persist_to_artifact_store, load_from_artifact_store, load_from_artifact_store_into_handle, StateVectorPersistRecord struct, STATE_VECTOR_PERSIST_ACTION_ID + STATE_VECTOR_PERSIST_WRITE_BOX_SCHEMA_ID constants; removed STATE_VECTOR_PERSIST_DISK_DEFERRED_MARKER and the deferral comment block)
src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/candle/mod.rs (extended pub use re-exports for the new MT-117 surface)
src/backend/handshake_core/src/kernel/action_catalog.rs (added subquadratic_persist_state_action and registered it in kernel002_action_catalog)
src/backend/handshake_core/tests/inf9_cross_session_state_tests.rs (extended with 11 disk-integration tests; removed the disk_integration_deferral_marker test)

edits_made.change: extended pub use re-exports to expose persist_to_bytes / load_from_bytes / load_into_handle / load_from_artifact_store / load_from_artifact_store_into_handle / persist_to_artifact_store / StateVectorPersistEnvelope / StateVectorPersistMetadata / StateVectorPersistRecord / STATE_VECTOR_PERSIST_ACTION_ID / STATE_VECTOR_PERSIST_WRITE_BOX_SCHEMA_ID. Original MT-117 prior session's bytes-in/bytes-out surface was also un-exported; without this the test file would not compile.
edits_made.why_unavoidable: Same defect class as the original MT-117 prior session — without the re-export the public surface is unreachable from integration tests. Test file's import line names these symbols directly under candle::, not candle::state_vector::.

edits_made.change: + subquadratic_persist_state_action() function (~50 lines) + 1-line registration in the kernel002_action_catalog actions vec
edits_made.why_unavoidable: MT-117 contract narrative names KernelActionCatalogV1 'kernel.subquadratic.persist_state' as the dispatch surface; without this action present in the kernel catalog the action_id constant in state_vector.rs would be orphaned. Same defect class as MT-145 memory_capsule_record_action which sat alongside its persistence implementation.

edits_avoided: No edits to ModelRuntime trait — disk persistence is engine-level (state_vector specific) not runtime-trait level.
No edits to WriteBoxKind enum — the catalog action declares ArtifactBox + state_vector_persist write_box schema_id via the existing Artifact variant; no new variant required.
No edits to ArtifactPayloadKind — used existing Bundle variant for the JSON envelope payload.
No edits to Tauri IPC commands — the operator-facing IPC bridge is a separate concern (downstream MT scope, follow-on UI work).

inf9_state_vector_prefix_reuse_tests_reg.note: MT-114 KvCacheOps-on-state-vector tests continue to pass after MT-117 surface additions

minimum_control_process_restart_simulati.evidence: Phase 1 commits snapshot via handle A + persists to disk; Phase 2 drops handle A + prefix_a; Phase 3 mints fresh handle B on same model+sha; Phase 4 loads envelope from artifact store via re-mint bridge; Phase 5 prefix_restore succeeds + handle B hit counter advances.

minimum_control_sha256_validation.evidence: Disk-path test persists snapshot against artifact_sha_a then attempts load_from_artifact_store_into_handle on a handle bound to artifact_sha_b; rejected with KvCacheError containing 'different model artifact' guidance.

minimum_control_variant_validation.evidence: Disk-path test persists Mamba2 snapshot then attempts load on an RWKV-v5 handle; rejected with KvCacheError containing 'variant mismatch'.

minimum_control_mutations_through_kernel.evidence: Tests pin the action_id constant ('kernel.subquadratic.persist_state') + write_box schema_id ('hsk.write_box.state_vector_persist@1') AND verify the action is registered in kernel002_action_catalog with the expected write_box, capabilities, and KERNEL_BUILDER role eligibility.

adversarial_tests_added_beyond_minimum_c: load_from_artifact_store_rejects_unknown_artifact_id — typed error with operator-readable guidance naming the missing artifact_root
load_from_artifact_store_rejects_tampered_payload_after_persist — manifest content_hash gate catches post-persist tampering before envelope parse runs
load_from_artifact_store_rejects_corrupted_manifest_json — manifest deserialize failure is typed (not panic)

persist_to_artifact_store_writes_into_l3_layer_under_workspace_root — pins the on-disk layout (L3 layer, bundle kind, medium classification, exportable=true) so a downstream MT adding a Layer/Classification mapping cannot silently drift the state-vector contract
persist_to_artifact_store_does_not_double_write_under_concurrent_calls — 4-thread concurrent persist of the same handle produces 4 distinct UUIDv7 artifact_ids; each loads back successfully
persist_to_artifact_store_rejects_export_from_unknown_prefix_handle — persist with an unbound KvPrefixHandle fails closed before write_file_artifact runs; no L3 artifact directory created
residual_risks: Tauri IPC bridge for kernel.subquadratic.persist_state action is NOT implemented in this MT — operator-facing IPC (so the UI can call persist_to_artifact_store from a button) is a follow-on UI MT. The action_id is registered in the catalog so a future IPC MT can wire it without changing the engine-side API.
POSTGRES_TEST_URL is not set in this session — but MT-117 disk persistence is filesystem-only (ArtifactStore L3 path), so no Postgres dependency. Tests fully exercised without Postgres.

Concurrent-persist test exercises in-process threading via std::thread. Tokio-runtime-driven concurrent persists are not separately tested because the surface is sync at the Rust level; if a future MT adds an async persist surface that test will need an async sibling.

envelope_version=v1 is pinned; if a future MT changes the SSMStateSnapshot enum variant set or adds a new tensor field, the envelope_version must bump and a migration shim must land. The pin test (persist_envelope_version_is_a_stable_string_constant) catches accidental bumps; intentional bumps require a paired migration test.
No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-117; supporting evidence only.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Process-restart simulation test (drop + reload + restore deterministic).

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: Cross-session state restore lands real disk persistence (persist_to_artifact_store + load_from_artifact_store + load_from_artifact_store_into_handle) via ArtifactStore with atomic write_file_artifact (temp+fsync+rename), manifest content_hash gate before envelope parse, sha256/variant validation on target handle, UUIDv7 artifact_ids, registered KernelActionCatalogV1 action 'kernel.subquadratic.persist_state' with write_box schema 'hsk.write_box.state_vector_persist@1'. 21 inf9_cross_session_state_tests PASS plus MT-114 regression (8 PASS). The Spec-Realism Gate sub-rules are correctly self-checked: sub_rule_1 (no deferred-live escape) — resumed the MT and implemented the prior session's deferral in-MT; sub_rule_2 (external resource touch) — real filesystem under .handshake/artifacts/L3/<uuid>/{payload,artifact.json}; sub_rule_3 (implementer cannot self-certify) — claimed_by ≠ validator role. All three red_team minimum controls covered with named tests and adversarial extensions. Operator E-2 'cross-session state restore' parity item: MET.
